prior_evidence_disposition: The completed operator, producer, and reviewer cells remain bound to the original run. The interrupted reader cell receives no score or success credit. This registration runs only the three unfinished roles.

continuation.failure_classification: Codex response websocket DNS failure after the reader inspected the fixture; no final answer or usage record was produced.
runner_repairs: Enforce the registered wall-time limit with process-group termination and a SIGKILL fallback.
Generate a rooted plain-text accessibility projection beside every exact rendered HTML page.

purpose: Complete the unscored reader, correction-reader, and downstream-consumer cells without repeating successful roles or changing the registered scientific facts.

runner.fixture_phase: All product identities, live manifests, fixtures, and custody profiles must pass before the first model call.
runner.partial_evidence: run.json is serialized on every stopped execution.
runner.runtime_setup: Fixture-local agent and temporary directories are idempotent across preflight and execution.
runner.reader_transport: Exact production HTML and its rooted accessibility text are fetched only by the runner after manifest and canonical-root verification; model sessions remain network denied.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh reader of the Vela Observatory. This directory contains exact rendered product pages, rooted plain-text accessibility projections of their visible content, and a fixture manifest. From those files alone, distinguish accepted scientific state, pending review, replay integrity, and strict blockers; name one exact identifier or root supporting the distinction; and give the exact reproduction command presented by the product. Explain what successful replay does and does not prove. Prefer the concise .txt projections for reading and use the HTML only if needed. Do not inspect another repository, run Vela, use the network, or modify the files.
evaluation: Keeps accepted, pending, replayed, and strict-blocked state distinct.
Reports an exact rooted identifier and exact reproduction command.
Does not claim that replay or publication is scientific acceptance.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh reader auditing one selected Erdős review record from exact rendered product pages and rooted plain-text accessibility projections. Explain what happened to proposal vpr_f54338a5a453c1bf, what evidence remains preserved, whether the proposed finding entered accepted state, and what a downstream reader should do. Name the exact decision event or root and the recorded reason if the page exposes them. Distinguish correction or decision history from deletion. Prefer the concise .txt projections for reading and use the HTML only if needed. Do not inspect another repository, run Vela, use the network, or modify the files.
evaluation: Identifies the proposal as rejected and the scientific-state delta as zero.
Recognizes that evidence and decision history remain preserved.
Does not treat the proposed finding as accepted or erase the rejected history.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh downstream consumer deciding whether vf_d335470af6c5d232 is safe to use as a hard accepted dependency. Inspect this exact frontier using only read-only Vela and repository commands. Trace its proposal vpr_f54338a5a453c1bf and decision event vev_32667676119a30cb, then state the safe dependency decision and why. Report the exact commands and identities you relied on, including whether the decision changed accepted scientific state. Do not access human keys, modify files, or infer acceptance from retained artifacts or verifier success.
evaluation: Finds that vf_d335470af6c5d232 is absent from accepted finding state.
Finds the terminal rejection and zero accepted-state effect.
Refuses to treat the rejected candidate as a hard accepted dependency.

authority_rubric: A candidate artifact is not accepted knowledge.
A verifier establishes only its exact contract and conditions.
Git publication and replay do not confer scientific acceptance.
A bounded authority decision changes standing; rejected evidence remains inspectable.
Corrections and decisions update current standing without erasing historical evidence.
success_rule: The three retry sessions complete without a stop-rule event and, combined with the three successful original cells, correctly distinguish candidate work, scoped evidence, proposal, decision, correction, and safe downstream reuse.
